background.text:
BACKGROUND
ND  
 
Tesoro Refinery  has applied to obtain an Authority to Construct (AC) and/or a Permit to Operate (PO) for alterations 
to the following equipment:  
 
S-1005   No 1 Hydrogen Plant  
S-1526  No 5 Gas Plant 
 
 
 
The S -1005 Hydrogen Plant will be altered to reroute start -up natural gas and hydrogen from the Low Temperature 
Shift Converter  to either the Feed Gas Suction KO Drum upstream of the Feedgas Compressor, or to the S -1526 No 
5 Gas Plant where it will be added to the fuel gas system.  This project will reduce flaring during planned startup of the S-1005 No. 1 Hydrogen Plant when the startup includes a catalyst reduction.  Natural gas and hydrogen are used 
to reduce (activate) the new LTS reactor catalyst every four to eight years.  The amount of flaring reduction is estimated to be about 12 MMSCFD during the startup period, typically lasting 2- 3 days (when the startup includes a 
catalyst reduction).  This proposed adjust ment will result in a significant reduction in flaring; but only during very 
infrequent events.  The project also reduces flaring during other unplanned No. 1 Hydrogen Plant shutdowns , when 
natural gas is used to warm -up the  LTS catalyst .   Most planned st artups do not require catalyst reduction or a rapid 
catalyst warm -up and no flaring occurs.  This project will have no effect on planned startups, which do not require 
the use of these gases and do not result in flaring . 
 
In the past, the effluent gases we re routed to the flare gas recovery system and flare.  This project will add two 
alternate destinations for the gases.  Piping will be added to recycle the gas back to the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant feed, 
as well as to, alternatively, route the gas to the fuel g as system.  When routed back to the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant feed, 
the recovered gas would be routed to the feed gas compressor suction and re -circulated back though the LTS in a 
continuous loop via the feed gas compressors.  This would back- out other natural gas and hydrogen usually fed to 
the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant.  When the reduction gases are routed to the 100 # Fuel Gas system mix pot, located at the 
No. 5 Gas Plant, it will back -out make -up natural gas otherwise required to supply the refinery and control the 
pressure on the 100# fuel gas header.   
 
Since this is just a startup activity, the project will not impact the process feed or production rate of the unit.  The 
sole purpose of the project is to reduce flare flow and flaring emissions and assist in meeting th e Consent Decree 
flaring limits of 1,010,902 scfd (365 -day average) and 1,516,353 scfd (30- day average).
 This project was included 

Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC  
Plant:  14628                                                                                                Application:  28936 
Page 2 of 4 as a Planned Reduction Measure in the October 2017 FMP Update  and if constructed will be identified  as a 
preventative measure  in future FMPs .BACKGROUND
ND  
 
Tesoro Refinery  has applied to obtain an Authority to Construct (AC) and/or a Permit to Operate (PO) for alterations 
to the following equipment:  
 
S-1005   No 1 Hydrogen Plant  
S-1526  No 5 Gas Plant 
 
 
 
The S -1005 Hydrogen Plant will be altered to reroute start -up natural gas and hydrogen from the Low Temperature 
Shift Converter  to either the Feed Gas Suction KO Drum upstream of the Feedgas Compressor, or to the S -1526 No 
5 Gas Plant where it will be added to the fuel gas system.  This project will reduce flaring during planned startup of the S-1005 No. 1 Hydrogen Plant when the startup includes a catalyst reduction.  Natural gas and hydrogen are used 
to reduce (activate) the new LTS reactor catalyst every four to eight years.  The amount of flaring reduction is estimated to be about 12 MMSCFD during the startup period, typically lasting 2- 3 days (when the startup includes a 
catalyst reduction).  This proposed adjust ment will result in a significant reduction in flaring; but only during very 
infrequent events.  The project also reduces flaring during other unplanned No. 1 Hydrogen Plant shutdowns , when 
natural gas is used to warm -up the  LTS catalyst .   Most planned st artups do not require catalyst reduction or a rapid 
catalyst warm -up and no flaring occurs.  This project will have no effect on planned startups, which do not require 
the use of these gases and do not result in flaring . 
 
In the past, the effluent gases we re routed to the flare gas recovery system and flare.  This project will add two 
alternate destinations for the gases.  Piping will be added to recycle the gas back to the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant feed, 
as well as to, alternatively, route the gas to the fuel g as system.  When routed back to the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant feed,

No. 5 Gas Plant, it will back -out make -up natural gas otherwise required to supply the refinery and control the

Since this is just a startup activity, the project will not impact the process feed or production rate of the unit.  The 
sole purpose of the project is to reduce flare flow and flaring emissions and assist in meeting th e Consent Decree

 This project was included 

Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC  
Plant:  14628                                                                                                Application:  28936

emission_calculations.text:
EMISSIONS
IONS  
 
The primary purpose of this project is to reduce flaring, so besides fugitive emissions, t here are no increase in 
emissions associated with this project.  If the startup gas is recycled to the Feed Gas Suction Drum then it is internally recycled reducing the natural gas required for the unit startup.  If the startup gas is sent to the fuel gas system, then it reduces the natural gas make -up required to satisfy the fuel gas system demand.  Tesoro has 
confirmed that the startup natural gas is unaffected by the startup process so there would be no emissions impact on the combustion sources associated with the fuel gas system.  
 Fugitive emission s are estimated based on the Tesoro component counts summarized below.

Emission Factor

Heavy Liquid  1.56E -04 0 0

toxic_risk_screening_analysis.narrative:
TOXIC RISK SCREENING ANALYSIS
The fugitive components are in natural gas service and the fugitive emissions are not expected to exceed any toxic triggers .  Therefore, a risk screen is not required . 
 
 
Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC  
Plant:  14628                                                                                                Application:  28936 
Page 3 of 4 BACT  
 
In accordance with Regulation 2 -2-301, BACT is triggered for any new or modified source with the potential to emit 
10 pounds or more per highest day of POC, NPOC, NOx, CO, SO 2, PM 2.5 or PM 10. 
 
The project permitted by this application  increases POC emissions by 0.724 lb/day.   Therefore, BACT does not 
apply.TOXIC RISK SCREENING ANALYSIS
YSIS

findings:
STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE  
 
There is no change to the compliance of S -1005 or S -1526.  Both sources are  process units  subject to, and are 
expected to comply, with Regulation 8, Rules 2 and 18.   
 
Since the project permitted by this application will facilitate compliance with the Teso ro Consent Decree, t his 
application is categorically exempt from CEQA pursuant to the District's Regulation 2 -1-312.3 : 
 
2-1-312 Other Categories of Exempt Projects: In addition to ministerial projects, the following categories 
of projects subject to permit  review by the District will be exempt from the CEQA review, either because the 
category is exempted by the express terms of CEQA (subsections 2 -1-312.1 through 312.9) or because the 
project has no potential for causing a significant adverse environmental impact (subsections 2-1-312.10 and 
312.11). Any permit applicant wishing to qualify under any of the specific exemptions set forth in this Section 
2-1-312 must include in its permit application CEQA -related information in accordance with subsection 2-1-
426.1. In addition, the CEQA -related information submitted by any permit applicant wishing to qualify under 
subsection 2-1-312.11 must demonstrate to the satisfaction of the APCO that the proposed project has no 
potential for resulting in a significant environmental effect in connection with any of the environmental media 
or resources listed in Section II of Appendix I of the State CEQA Guidelines.  
 312.3 Permit applications for projects undertaken for the sole purpose of bringing an existing 
facility into compliance with newly adopted regulatory requirements of the District or of any other local, 
state or federal agency.  
 Tesoro has completed Form Appendix H in support of this CEQA exemption.  
 NSPS, NESHAPS, BACT, Toxics  and PSD are not applicable. 
 This fac ility is located greater than 1,000 feet from the nearest school and therefore is not subject to the public 
notification requirements of Regulation 2 -1-412.STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE  
 
There is no change to the compliance of S -1005 or S -1526.  Both sources are  process units  subject to, and are 
expected to comply, with Regulation 8, Rules 2 and 18.

conditions.text:
PERMIT CONDITIONS
IONS 
 The following permit conditions will be imposed by this permit application.  These conditions only address fugitive 
emissions.  All other permit conditions required by the Consent Decree will be addressed in Tesoro Application 
28578.  
Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC  
Plant:  14628                                                                                                Application:  28936 
Page 4 of 4  
 
Tesoro Refining & Ma rketing Co, LLC, Plant 14628  
S-1005 No 1 HydrogenPlant  
S-1526 No 5 Gas Plant  
No 1 Hydrogen Plant Startup Flare Reduction Project  
Application 289 36 (March  2018) 
 
 
1. Not more than 30 days after the installation of the S -1005 and S -1526 Flare Reduction Project, the 
owner/operator shall provide the District's Engineering Division with a final count of fugitive components installed. The owner/operator has been permitted for a total increase in the following 
fugitive components:  
56 valves in gas service  
83 flanges in gas service  
(basis: Cumulative Increase, offsets)   
 
2. The owne r/operator shall not exceed 0.13 2 tons per year of POC emissions measured as C1 from the 
total fugitive component count installed as part of Application 28936.   (basis: Cumulative Incr ease, 
offsets)   
 
3. If there is an increase in the total fugitive component emissions, the plant's cumulative emissions for 
the project shall be adjusted to reflect the difference between emissions based on predicted versus actual component counts. The owner /operator shall provide to the District all additional required 
offsets at an offset ratio of 1.15:1 no later than 14 days after submittal of the final POC fugitive count. If the actual component count is less than the predicted, the total will be adjusted  accordingly and all 
emission offsets applied by the owner/operator in excess of the actual total fugitive emissions will be 
credited back to the owner/operator. (basis: offsets)  
 
4. In accordance with the provisions of Regulation 8- 18, the owner/operator sha ll integrate all new 
fugitive equipment in organic service installed as part of Application 28936 into the facility fugitive 
equipment monitoring and repair program. (basis: BACT, Regulation 8- 18)PERMIT CONDITIONS
IONS 
 The following permit conditions will be imposed by this permit application.  These conditions only address fugitive 
emissions.  All other permit conditions required by the Consent Decree will be addressed in Tesoro Application 
28578.  
Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC  
Plant:  14628                                                                                                Application:  28936 
Page 4 of 4  
 
Tesoro Refining & Ma rketing Co, LLC, Plant 14628  
S-1005 No 1 HydrogenPlant  
S-1526 No 5 Gas Plant  
No 1 Hydrogen Plant Startup Flare Reduction Project  
Application 289 36 (March  2018) 
 
 
1. Not more than 30 days after the installation of the S -1005 and S -1526 Flare Reduction Project, the 
owner/operator shall provide the District's Engineering Division with a final count of fugitive components installed. The owner/operator has been permitted for a total increase in the following 
fugitive components:  
56 valves in gas service  
83 flanges in gas service  
(basis: Cumulative Increase, offsets)   
 
2. The owne r/operator shall not exceed 0.13 2 tons per year of POC emissions measured as C1 from the 
total fugitive component count installed as part of Application 28936.   (basis: Cumulative Incr ease, 
offsets)   
 
3. If there is an increase in the total fugitive component emissions, the plant's cumulative emissions for 
the project shall be adjusted to reflect the difference between emissions based on predicted versus actual component counts. The owner /operator shall provide to the District all additional required 
offsets at an offset ratio of 1.15:1 no later than 14 days after submittal of the final POC fugitive count. If the actual component count is less than the predicted, the total will be adjusted  accordingly and all 
emission offsets applied by the owner/operator in excess of the actual total fugitive emissions will be 
credited back to the owner/operator. (basis: offsets)  
 
4. In accordance with the provisions of Regulation 8- 18, the owner/operator sha ll integrate all new 
fugitive equipment in organic service installed as part of Application 28936 into the facility fugitive 
equipment monitoring and repair program. (basis: BACT, Regulation 8- 18)

recommendation.text:
RECOMMENDATIONRECOMMENDATION
ENDATION  
 It is recommended d that Tesoro Refinery  be issued an Authority to Construct f or the following alteration : 
 
S-1005   No 1 Hydrogen Plant  
S-1526  No 5 Gas Plant 
Installation of the piping and valves required for the Hydrogen Plant Startup Flare Reduction Measure  
 
   
Prepared by:  ______________________________  Date:  _____________________ 
Art Valla , Senior Air Quality Engineer   March  21, 2018

blocks.heading: STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
blocks.text:
STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE  
 
There is no change to the compliance of S -1005 or S -1526.  Both sources are  process units  subject to, and are 
expected to comply, with Regulation 8, Rules 2 and 18.   
 
Since the project permitted by this application will facilitate compliance with the Teso ro Consent Decree, t his 
application is categorically exempt from CEQA pursuant to the District's Regulation 2 -1-312.3 : 
 
2-1-312 Other Categories of Exempt Projects: In addition to ministerial projects, the following categories 
of projects subject to permit  review by the District will be exempt from the CEQA review, either because the 
category is exempted by the express terms of CEQA (subsections 2 -1-312.1 through 312.9) or because the 
project has no potential for causing a significant adverse environmental impact (subsections 2-1-312.10 and 
312.11). Any permit applicant wishing to qualify under any of the specific exemptions set forth in this Section 
2-1-312 must include in its permit application CEQA -related information in accordance with subsection 2-1-
426.1. In addition, the CEQA -related information submitted by any permit applicant wishing to qualify under 
subsection 2-1-312.11 must demonstrate to the satisfaction of the APCO that the proposed project has no 
potential for resulting in a significant environmental effect in connection with any of the environmental media 
or resources listed in Section II of Appendix I of the State CEQA Guidelines.  
 312.3 Permit applications for projects undertaken for the sole purpose of bringing an existing 
facility into compliance with newly adopted regulatory requirements of the District or of any other local, 
state or federal agency.  
 Tesoro has completed Form Appendix H in support of this CEQA exemption.  
 NSPS, NESHAPS, BACT, Toxics  and PSD are not applicable. 
 This fac ility is located greater than 1,000 feet from the nearest school and therefore is not subject to the public 
notification requirements of Regulation 2 -1-412.

blocks.heading: PERMIT CONDITIONS
blocks.text:
PERMIT CONDITIONS
IONS 
 The following permit conditions will be imposed by this permit application.  These conditions only address fugitive 
emissions.  All other permit conditions required by the Consent Decree will be addressed in Tesoro Application 
28578.  
Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC  
Plant:  14628                                                                                                Application:  28936 
Page 4 of 4  
 
Tesoro Refining & Ma rketing Co, LLC, Plant 14628  
S-1005 No 1 HydrogenPlant  
S-1526 No 5 Gas Plant  
No 1 Hydrogen Plant Startup Flare Reduction Project  
Application 289 36 (March  2018) 
 
 
1. Not more than 30 days after the installation of the S -1005 and S -1526 Flare Reduction Project, the 
owner/operator shall provide the District's Engineering Division with a final count of fugitive components installed. The owner/operator has been permitted for a total increase in the following 
fugitive components:  
56 valves in gas service  
83 flanges in gas service  
(basis: Cumulative Increase, offsets)   
 
2. The owne r/operator shall not exceed 0.13 2 tons per year of POC emissions measured as C1 from the 
total fugitive component count installed as part of Application 28936.   (basis: Cumulative Incr ease, 
offsets)   
 
3. If there is an increase in the total fugitive component emissions, the plant's cumulative emissions for 
the project shall be adjusted to reflect the difference between emissions based on predicted versus actual component counts. The owner /operator shall provide to the District all additional required 
offsets at an offset ratio of 1.15:1 no later than 14 days after submittal of the final POC fugitive count. If the actual component count is less than the predicted, the total will be adjusted  accordingly and all 
emission offsets applied by the owner/operator in excess of the actual total fugitive emissions will be 
credited back to the owner/operator. (basis: offsets)  
 
4. In accordance with the provisions of Regulation 8- 18, the owner/operator sha ll integrate all new 
fugitive equipment in organic service installed as part of Application 28936 into the facility fugitive 
equipment monitoring and repair program. (basis: BACT, Regulation 8- 18)

blocks.heading: RECOMMENDATION
blocks.text: RECOMMENDATION

blocks.heading: BACKGROUND
blocks.text:
BACKGROUND
ND  
 
Tesoro Refinery  has applied to obtain an Authority to Construct (AC) and/or a Permit to Operate (PO) for alterations 
to the following equipment:  
 
S-1005   No 1 Hydrogen Plant  
S-1526  No 5 Gas Plant 
 
 
 
The S -1005 Hydrogen Plant will be altered to reroute start -up natural gas and hydrogen from the Low Temperature 
Shift Converter  to either the Feed Gas Suction KO Drum upstream of the Feedgas Compressor, or to the S -1526 No 
5 Gas Plant where it will be added to the fuel gas system.  This project will reduce flaring during planned startup of the S-1005 No. 1 Hydrogen Plant when the startup includes a catalyst reduction.  Natural gas and hydrogen are used 
to reduce (activate) the new LTS reactor catalyst every four to eight years.  The amount of flaring reduction is estimated to be about 12 MMSCFD during the startup period, typically lasting 2- 3 days (when the startup includes a 
catalyst reduction).  This proposed adjust ment will result in a significant reduction in flaring; but only during very 
infrequent events.  The project also reduces flaring during other unplanned No. 1 Hydrogen Plant shutdowns , when 
natural gas is used to warm -up the  LTS catalyst .   Most planned st artups do not require catalyst reduction or a rapid 
catalyst warm -up and no flaring occurs.  This project will have no effect on planned startups, which do not require 
the use of these gases and do not result in flaring . 
 
In the past, the effluent gases we re routed to the flare gas recovery system and flare.  This project will add two 
alternate destinations for the gases.  Piping will be added to recycle the gas back to the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant feed, 
as well as to, alternatively, route the gas to the fuel g as system.  When routed back to the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant feed, 
the recovered gas would be routed to the feed gas compressor suction and re -circulated back though the LTS in a 
continuous loop via the feed gas compressors.  This would back- out other natural gas and hydrogen usually fed to 
the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant.  When the reduction gases are routed to the 100 # Fuel Gas system mix pot, located at the 
No. 5 Gas Plant, it will back -out make -up natural gas otherwise required to supply the refinery and control the 
pressure on the 100# fuel gas header.   
 
Since this is just a startup activity, the project will not impact the process feed or production rate of the unit.  The 
sole purpose of the project is to reduce flare flow and flaring emissions and assist in meeting th e Consent Decree 
flaring limits of 1,010,902 scfd (365 -day average) and 1,516,353 scfd (30- day average).
 This project was included 

Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC  
Plant:  14628                                                                                                Application:  28936 
Page 2 of 4 as a Planned Reduction Measure in the October 2017 FMP Update  and if constructed will be identified  as a 
preventative measure  in future FMPs .

blocks.heading: EMISSIONS
blocks.text:
EMISSIONS
IONS  
 
The primary purpose of this project is to reduce flaring, so besides fugitive emissions, t here are no increase in 
emissions associated with this project.  If the startup gas is recycled to the Feed Gas Suction Drum then it is internally recycled reducing the natural gas required for the unit startup.  If the startup gas is sent to the fuel gas system, then it reduces the natural gas make -up required to satisfy the fuel gas system demand.  Tesoro has 
confirmed that the startup natural gas is unaffected by the startup process so there would be no emissions impact on the combustion sources associated with the fuel gas system.
